channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Parade of victims: Democrats invite Epstein survivors, deportation targets as foils to Trump speech
item.description: As President Trump surveys his audience at the U.S. Capitol on Tuesday night, he'll see some friends in the gallery -- but also plenty of opponents, i...

item.title: The AI leadership reckoning is here
item.description: Your CEO is in danger and generational change is afoot.

item.title: New York Democrats Demand ID To Shovel Snow But Not To Vote
item.description: A new Heritage Action poll further confirms voters' overwhelming support for proof of citizenship and ID to vote.

item.title: Obtuse GOP Politicians Are Pretending The Courts Will Save Us
item.description: There is no evidence of any kind that Supreme Court rulings will stop government from continuing to get much bigger. This kind of make-believe is fata...

item.title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy says sucking up to your boss won’t earn their trust
item.description: Want to earn the trust of your boss and peers? Sucking up to them won’t do the trick, says Amazon’s CEO Andy Jassy.

item.title: Despite Promise To Be Pro-Life ‘Ally,’ Trump Admin Won’t Regulate Abortion Drug Endangering Women
item.description: The FDA appears to be slow-walking its mifepristone review, frustrating Trump allies in both Congress and pro-life states.

item.title: To go or not to go? Supreme Court at the State of the Union
item.description: Supreme Court justices face a decision on attending President Trump's State of the Union address after he said was "ashamed" of six who voted against ...

item.title: Jack Hughes or Eileen Gu?
item.description: Is loyalty to country a matter of choice, or an unalterable commitment?

item.title: Steve Jobs adopted a no ‘bozos’ policy and said the best managers are those who never wanted the job—here are his 3 best management tips
item.description: Hiring only for “professional management” doesn’t work, Steve Jobs said.

item.title: Gavin Newsom Is Playing A ‘Trump’ Character He Doesn’t Understand
item.description: Newsom and his communications team see the form: Trump insults people and he's rude, so he won the presidency, so being rude and insulting wins the pr...

item.title: Growing number of Democrats skipping Trump's 2026 State of the Union
item.description: Democratic lawmakers are split on attending Donald Trump's State of the Union Tuesday, with House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries offering options of ...

item.title: What Is a ‘Conservative Movie’?
item.description: Our cultural blind spot.

item.title: ‘It’s going to be painful for a lot of people’: Software engineers could go extinct this year, says Claude Code creator
item.description: Boris Cherny says Claude Code has been writing all of his code since November.

item.title: Report card: Conservative and liberal strategists reveal how Trump has fared on campaign promises
item.description: Ahead of President Donald Trump's first State of the Union address since returning to the White House, his record on key campaign pledges is mixed.

item.title: Federal court refuses to block new Utah congressional voting map that may favor Democrats
item.description: A court refused to block a new Utah redistricting map which creates a Democrat-heavy Salt Lake City district, potentially flipping a GOP-held House se...

item.title: Europe’s War on Digital Speech
item.description: The continent should back down on its overregulation of innovative tech companies.

item.title: Hockey Loss Should Awaken Canada To Its Long-Running Anti-American Decline
item.description: Canada lost to the United States on the largest stage and in the one domain in which its superiority felt metaphysically guaranteed.

item.title: Supreme Court Tariff Ruling May Not Be Good News for China
item.description: In the long run, the reaffirmation of American constitutional governance could strengthen U.S. alliances.

item.title: The Duty to Say What the Law Is
item.description: February 24 is not merely an anniversary. It is a reminder that constitutional structure endures only if it is defended.

item.title: Former UK ambassador to US released on bail after arrest in Epstein misconduct investigation
item.description: Former U.K. Ambassador to the U.S. Peter Mandelson was released on bail after being arrested for suspicion of misconduct in public office, reports ind...

item.title: Viewer's guide to President Trump's 2026 State of the Union: How to watch and what to listen for
item.description: President Trump will deliver the first formal State of the Union address of his second term tonight before a joint session of Congress at the U.S. Cap...

item.title: Trump sues N.J. Gov. Mikie Sherill over new sanctuary policy
item.description: The Trump administration has sued to try to stop New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherill's new sanctuary policy that bars federal officers from staging on state...

item.title: Maryland Delegate Nicole Williams running to replace Rep. Steny H. Hoyer
item.description: Maryland state Delegate Nicole Williams has announced her candidacy to succeed Rep. Steny H. Hoyer after his 46-year tenure in representing the state'...

item.title: Democrats dare again to dream in Texas' U.S. Senate race
item.description: The last time a Democrat won statewide in Texas was in 1994. Jeff Bezos was just launching Amazon. The sitcom "Friends" was brand new. The image of O....

item.title: Anthropic claims 3 Chinese companies ripped it off, using its AI tools to train their models: ‘How the turn tables’
item.description: Anthropic, which settled a copyright claim by thousands of authors for $1.5 billion months ago, claims the Chinese firms used "distillation" technique...

item.title: Building Commitment to Long-Term Investing
item.description: Why clients stall on long-term investing and how reframing commitment around flexibility unlocks forward momentum. The post Building Commitment to Lon...

item.title: Why Static Portfolios Fail When Risk Regimes Change
item.description: Why static portfolio frameworks fail when risk regimes shift, drawing lessons from the very different market breakdowns of 2020 and 2022. The post Why...

item.title: What the Market Knows That WACC Doesn’t
item.description: Market-implied discount rates reveal how investors price risk, often diverging from WACC and reshaping capital decisions. The post What the Market Kno...

item.title: Attention Bias in AI-Driven Investing
item.description: AI tools may favor popular stocks over overlooked ones, embedding attention bias into investment decisions. The post Attention Bias in AI-Driven Inves...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...
